title: adiantum caudatum var. capillus-gorgonis
intro: Adiantum caudatum var. capillus-gorgonis, also known as the 'Queen Alexandra's Rainbow Fern', is a rare and exotic variety of the Adiantum caudatum plant, which belongs to the maidenhair fern family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. The health benefits of Adiantum caudatum var. capillus-gorgonis include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by steeping the fronds in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
